howToPlayStep3: Click the Play button to start the draw
howToPlayStep4: Watch as the lottery simulates drawings until your numbers match
howToPlayStep5: See how many draws it takes!
oddsDescription: Your chance of winning this game on any single draw is approximately 1 in {{odds}}
oddsNote: This is why understanding lottery odds matters for your financial health!

results.fact: This is why lottery odds matter - on average, you need {{draws}} draws to win the jackpot!

perspective.title: Putting the Odds in Perspective
perspective.howLongDesc: If you played {{name}} regularly, here's how long you'd expect to wait for a single win:
perspective.howMuchSpendDesc: At $2 per ticket, here's what you'd expect to spend before winning once:
perspective.perYearTwiceWeek: per year playing twice a week
perspective.perYearOnceWeek: per year playing once a week
perspective.lightningDesc: The odds of being struck by lightning in a given year are roughly 1 in 1,222,000.
perspective.lightningLabel: more likely to be struck by lightning than to win {{name}}
perspective.coinFlipDesc: Winning {{name}} is as likely as flipping a coin and getting heads every single time:
perspective.coinFlipLabel: consecutive heads needed (each flip halves your chances)
perspective.ticketStackDesc: If each lottery ticket were 1mm thick and you stacked all the tickets needed to expect one win:
perspective.moonDistance: of the distance to the Moon (384,400 km)
perspective.pickCardDesc: A standard deck has 52 cards. Winning {{name}} is like picking one specific card from a huge pile:
perspective.pickCardLabel: shuffle {{decks}} decks together and draw the Ace of Spades on your first try
perspective.lifetimesDesc: Assuming an average human lifespan of 80 years, here's how many lifetimes you'd need:
perspective.birthdayDesc: The chance of a random stranger sharing your exact birthday is 1 in 365. Winning {{name}} is like:
perspective.birthdayLabel: meeting {{count}} random people and ALL of them sharing your exact birthday
